title: Around the Corner Bookshelf
summary: A modular bookshelf system that starts as a standalone unit and can be extended with a corner add-on panel — same trim and shelf language on both, wrapping continuously around a room corner.
description:
This bookshelf is designed to be built once as a standalone unit and extended later — the main bookshelf stands on its own, complete with its own top and shelves, but a matching side panel and shelf set can be added afterward to wrap the whole thing around a room corner. Build the main unit first exactly like any standalone bookshelf: attach 1x2 trim to the side panels, add legs, then top supports, then the trimmed shelf, then the top.

**The corner add-on reuses the main unit's side panel as its own shared wall** rather than building two full independent bookshelves side by side — its own third side panel, legs, and shelves all attach directly onto the completed main unit. This is what makes the two pieces read as one continuous wraparound shelf rather than two separate boxes pushed together in a corner.

A back panel is optional on both sections and goes on last, nailed and glued into place once every shelf and trim piece is set. If you only want the standalone version, stop after the main bookshelf steps — the corner add-on is a genuinely separate phase, not a required finishing step.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut four main side trim at 11-1/4", two main middle shelf trim at 29-1/4", two main top trim at 32-3/4", two corner add-on side panel trim at 11-1/4" and two corner add-on top trim at 18-1/2" from the 1x2. Cut two main side panels at 26-1/2", two main middle/bottom shelves at 29-1/4", one main top at 32-3/4", one corner add-on side panel at 26-1/2", two corner add-on bottom/middle shelves at 16-1/2" and one corner add-on top at 18-1/2" from the 1x12. Cut four main legs at 29", four main top/bottom trim at 29-1/4", two corner add-on legs at 29" and four corner add-on top/bottom trim at 16-1/2" from the 2x2. Cut one back panel, main at 27" from the 1/4" plywood, for the backs (optional). Cut one back panel, corner add-on at 27" from the 1/4" plywood, for the backs (optional). Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to three 8 ft 1x2, two 10 ft 1x12 and four 8 ft 2x2. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Build the main side panels and legs
steps.body: Each side assembly is one 26-1/2" x 11-1/4" panel with an 11-1/4" 1x2 trim across its top edge and another across its bottom, then a 29" 2x2 leg at the front edge and another at the back. Drill 3/4" pocket holes along the panel's front and back edges before the legs go on, and attach each leg so its INSIDE face is flush with the inside face of the panel — the leg then stands proud on the outside. That flush inside face is the reference for the whole unit: it puts the two assemblies 29-1/4" apart, which is exactly the length every shelf and trim piece is cut to. Build both side assemblies identically and stack them to check before going on.

steps.title: Add top and bottom trim and the middle shelf
steps.body: Stand the two side assemblies parallel, 29-1/4" apart inside face to inside face, and join them with the four 29-1/4" 2x2 top and bottom trims — two across the top, front and back, and two across the bottom. Then glue and pin a 29-1/4" 1x2 middle shelf trim to the front edge of each 29-1/4" shelf and fasten the trimmed shelves between the panels with pocket hole screws driven from underneath so nothing shows. Check the diagonals as you go: with a 29-1/4" span every part is cut to one number, so a rack of even 1/8" makes the last shelf fight.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Glue and pin the two 32-3/4" 1x2 top trims to the front and back edges of the 32-3/4" top, then set the top on the case. The unit measures 32-1/4" across the outside faces of the legs, so a 32-3/4" top centered on it leaves a 1/4" reveal at each end — take that off both ends rather than letting it all land on one side. Fasten down through pocket holes in the top supports so the top face stays clean. The standalone main bookshelf is complete at this point; stop here if you do not want the corner section.

steps.title: Build and set the corner add-on side panel
steps.body: Build the add-on's third side panel exactly like the main ones: a 26-1/2" panel with two 11-1/4" 1x2 trims and a 29" 2x2 leg flush to its inside face at each edge. Stand it 16-1/2" clear of the main unit's side panel, at right angles to the main unit's run so the two sections wrap the corner. It does not attach to the main unit directly — 16-1/2" of air separates them — so brace it plumb for now and let the trims in the next step make the connection.

steps.title: Tie the add-on together with trims, shelves and top
steps.body: The four 16-1/2" 2x2 top and bottom trims are what actually joins the add-on to the main unit: two across the top and two across the bottom, running from the new side panel to the main unit's side, fastened with pocket hole screws at both ends. Then add the two 16-1/2" shelves in the same order as the main unit, and finish with the 18-1/2" top carrying its two 18-1/2" 1x2 trims. Keep the add-on's shelf heights level with the main unit's — matching lines are what makes the two sections read as one continuous shelf rather than two boxes shoved into a corner.

steps.title: Attach the backs and finish
steps.body: The two optional 1/4" backs are both 27" tall but different widths, and the cut list's labels look reversed: the 31-1/2" panel matches the main unit's 32-1/4" width, and the 19" panel matches the add-on's 16-1/2" bay. Hold each panel up to its opening before nailing and use whichever actually fits. Glue and pin them on with 1-1/4" brads, checking the case for square first — a back panel locks in whatever shape the case is in when it goes on. Then sand every surface and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Modular bookshelf wrapping around a room corner
